eventMetadata.context_description: Charles Leclerc topped the sole FP1 session at Miami International Autodrome with a 1:29.855 lap, 0.164s clear of Max Verstappen, highlighting Ferrari's heavy upgrades and strong single-lap pace on the tight, low-speed layout. Championship leader Kimi Antonelli, fresh off wins in China and Japan, briefly led early on hard tires before a power unit glitch aborted his soft run, dropping him to P5 and affecting both Mercedes drivers amid reliability concerns. Traders' near-even implied probabilities for Antonelli (36.5%) and Leclerc (34.5%) reflect this razor-thin battle, with George Russell's P6 underscoring Mercedes' qualifying pedigree despite woes, while Lando Norris and Verstappen trail as realistic threats in the impending sprint shootout knockout segments.

markets.question: Will Hunter Brown strike out the most batters during the 2026 MLB regular season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the pitcher who records the most strikeouts among pitchers during the 2026 Major League Baseball regular season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by the rules of the MLB. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the pitcher that records fewer innings pitched during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that records the lower ERA during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, then this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that walked fewer batters during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the pitcher whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MLB; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Democratic victory at 92.5¢ in Pennsylvania's 6th Congressional District House race, reflecting incumbent Rep. Chrissy Houlahan's commanding position in a Solid Democratic seat with Cook PVI D+6. Houlahan, an Air Force veteran seeking a fifth term, secured 56% in 2024 and larger margins previously against underfunded challengers, buoyed by Chester County's suburban, college-educated voters who have trended reliably blue. With March filing deadlines passed, her May 19 primary faces no serious opposition, while Republican Marty Young enters without national buzz or fundraising edge. Odds hold firm absent a Houlahan scandal, primary upset, or strong national Republican midterm wave tilting battleground Pennsylvania.

markets.question: Will Drake feature Lil Durk on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed artists who feature on Drake's album "ICEMAN".

To qualify as "featured", the listed artist must be credited on at least one song on the album according to at least one major streaming platform: namely Spotify, Apple Music, Amazon Music, or YouTube Music.

If the album fails to release by December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source of this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Drake's ninth studio album *Iceman*, confirmed for release on May 15 via OVO Sound and Republic Records, drives intense speculation in feature markets amid a cryptic rollout featuring livestream episodes and Toronto ice installations. No official tracklist or additional guest credits have been announced beyond historical OVO patterns with affiliates like 21 Savage, Future, and PARTYNEXTDOOR, though cross-genre buzz surrounds UK drill star Central Cee and rage rapper Yeat from rumored singles. Streamer Adin Ross, claiming insider knowledge, dismissed top speculated names including Central Cee, Sexyy Red, and Lil Wayne on April 30, injecting uncertainty just two weeks from drop. Traders eye pre-release leaks or last-minute singles as key catalysts, given Drake's track record of surprise collabs in a competitive summer rap landscape.

eventMetadata.context_description: In New York's 21st Congressional District, trader consensus favors Republicans at 64% implied probability to retain the open seat vacated by Elise Stefanik, reflecting the district's R+10 structural lean and historical GOP dominance in recent cycles. Anthony Constantino leads the Republican primary field with strong early polling—including a February survey by Stefanik's pollster showing his massive advantage—superior fundraising, and a pivotal April 21 endorsement from President Trump that overrides the state party's backing of Assemblyman Robert Smullen. Democrats remain fragmented post-Dylan Hewitt's March dropout, with no clear frontrunner ahead of the June 23 closed primaries, contributing to their 32% odds amid limited general election polling. The general election is November 3.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tarik Skubal leads Polymarket's 2026 AL Cy Young trader consensus at 21.5% implied probability, buoyed by his back-to-back prior wins, elite 2.70 ERA, 0.95 WHIP, and No. 1 ranking in MLB's first-month poll despite a 3-2 record through seven starts for the Tigers. Jose Soriano's breakout surge to 12.7% reflects his dominant 5-1 mark, 0.84 ERA, and 49 strikeouts across 42.2 innings for the Angels, including multiple double-digit K gems and a skyrocketing whiff rate. Close behind, Dylan Cease (12.3%) boasts league-leading strikeouts post-Blue Jays signing, while rookie Cam Schlittler (11.9%) tops early ERA charts at 0.00 for the Yankees, fueling a wide-open field amid strong rotations and no major injury reports.

markets.question: Will an Iranian agent be charged in the US by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or announces a criminal indictment of any individual alleged to be acting as an agent of the Iranian government between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Iranian agent” refers to any individual alleged to be acting on behalf of, under the direction of, or in coordination with the Iranian government (including its military or intelligence services) to conduct espionage, collect intelligence, or perform other covert operations that benefit Iran.

For the purposes of this market, the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors "No" at 78.5% implied probability for an Iranian agent—defined as someone formally charged with acting at the direction of the Iranian government—being indicted by May 31, reflecting the absence of fresh DOJ actions since the April 20 federal complaint against Shamim Mafi for conspiring to broker arms sales on behalf of Iran's government and IRGC, including 62 contacts with an Iranian intelligence officer. Subsequent developments, like the April 24 unsealing of migrant smuggling charges against Jafar Tafakori, lack allegations of government direction. With three weeks remaining and no announced investigations or hearings, the sporadic pace of prior cases, such as the March conviction of Asif Merchant in a murder-for-hire plot, underpins skepticism for another timely indictment amid ongoing U.S. sanctions enforcement.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 21°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory observations confirm the city's highest temperature on April 30, 2026, reached 26.7°C, driving the market's unanimous 100% implied probability for 25°C or higher as traders align with this verified measurement from the official King’s Park station. This outcome fits an exceptionally warm April, marked by abundant sunshine and highs exceeding 30°C mid-month, amid forecasts of above-normal temperatures linked to persistent high-pressure systems and urban heat effects. Historical April averages hover around 26–27°C late in the month, reinforcing climatological norms. With data finalized via standard meteorological protocols, challenges would require rare audit revisions or sensor anomalies, though none are indicated in preliminary reports. Traders await no further updates, as resolution criteria are met.

markets.question: Will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit (HIGH) $304 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve maintained its federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% unchanged following the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting—the third consecutive hold—despite March CPI inflation surging to 3.3% year-over-year on a 0.9% monthly rise, exceeding forecasts, while unemployment held steady at 4.3%. The effective rate lingers at 3.64%, with Fed funds futures implying trader consensus for a mostly flat path through 2026 amid balanced risks to growth and price stability. Polymarket sentiment reflects skin-in-the-game bets on modest easing before year-end 2027 if disinflation materializes, though policy dissent signals debate. Watch April CPI on May 12 and June 16-17 FOMC projections for shifts in rate expectations.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Joe Wilson's dominant fundraising—$294,000 cash on hand as of late March—and history of double-digit general election victories, including 59.5% in 2024, anchor trader consensus at 82% implied probability for a Republican Party win in the safely red SC-02 district, rated Solid Republican by Cook Political Report with an R+7 partisan lean where Trump won by 14 points last year. The June 9 Republican primary pits Wilson against challengers Hamp Redmond and Sam Gibbons, but his incumbency and prior 73.9% primary margin position him as heavy favorite. Democrats hold 18% amid a fragmented primary field featuring 2024 nominee David Robinson II, Zyon Khalifa, Roger Pruitt, and Daniel Shrief, with no recent polling or catalysts post-March 30 filing deadline to shift dynamics ahead of the November 3 general election. Close pricing on leading candidate-specific outcomes A, B, and Other around 50% reflects nominee uncertainty.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 44% implied probability to a high temperature of 68-69°F at Los Angeles International Airport on May 2, driven by the National Weather Service's latest guidance forecasting persistent marine layer stratus clouds and patchy morning fog under light onshore winds of 5-10 mph, which promote a low-level temperature inversion capping daytime heating near coastal sites. Lower odds for 70°F+ reflect low clearing potential, though evening NWS forecast discussions and 00Z model updates could refine trajectories amid inherent short-term forecast uncertainty.

markets.question: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 11 or more kills than Weibo Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Weibo Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's recent threats to withdraw the United States from NATO, amid allies' refusal to join the US-Israel military action against Iran, have fueled speculation, with White House spokeswoman Caroline Leavitt confirming internal discussions as recently as early April 2026. However, trader consensus reflects deep skepticism due to statutory barriers in Section 1250A of the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act, which prohibits unilateral presidential withdrawal without congressional approval or a two-thirds Senate vote. Bipartisan opposition from senators like Tim Kaine and Republicans underscores institutional resistance, while NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte deemed an exit unlikely in mid-April. No formal steps have advanced, and upcoming congressional sessions or diplomatic summits could clarify commitments before any 2027 deadline.

markets.description:
The Jeonnam–Gwangju mayoral election is scheduled to take place on June 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Interim, temporary, or caretaker mayors will not count.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission (https://www.nec.go.kr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Min Hyung-bae's commanding trader consensus stems from the Democratic Party of Korea's longstanding dominance in the Honam region's local elections, where it routinely secures supermajorities, positioning him as the near-lock for the June 3 inaugural Jeonnam-Gwangju Integrated Special City mayor. He clinched the party's nomination on April 14 after a hard-fought primary runoff against incumbent Jeonnam Governor Kim Yung-rok, buoyed by consistent polling leads in pre-runoff surveys showing him ahead by 10-13 points among party voters. People Power Party's Lee Jung-hyun, nominated April 22, trails at minimal odds despite pledging a "30% election revolution" to challenge the monopoly; historical precedents show conservative candidates garnering under 20% in similar races. Recent minor controversy over primary ARS outages was dismissed by the party. Upsets would require a major scandal, health issue for Min, or national wave shifting turnout dramatically in the final month.

eventMetadata.context_description: Harry Kane leads trader consensus for the 2026 Ballon d'Or at 36% implied probability, propelled by his Bundesliga-leading 33 goals and total of 45 across competitions during Bayern Munich's title-clinching 4-2 win over Stuttgart on April 19, securing a record 35th German crown. Teammate Michael Olise follows at 19%, his 13 goals and 18 assists forming a lethal front three with Kane that produced 149 combined goal contributions, highlighted in the dramatic 4-3 comeback at Mainz on April 25. Kylian Mbappé sits third at 12% after Real Madrid's quarterfinal exit to Bayern, while Ousmane Dembélé and Lamine Yamal trail amid PSG's 5-4 UCL semifinal first-leg edge and Barcelona's solid but trophyless campaign. Bayern's DFB-Pokal final berth and looming Champions League second leg keep the race fluid.

markets.question: PEC Zwolle vs. Heracles Almelo: O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Eredivisie game between PEC Zwolle and Heracles Almelo, scheduled for May 3 at 8:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if PEC Zwolle and Heracles Almelo combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on eredivisie.nl. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: PEC Zwolle vs. Heracles Almelo - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for May 3 at 8:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: PEC Zwolle host relegation-threatened Heracles Almelo on May 3 at MAC³PARK Stadion in a crucial Eredivisie clash, with Zwolle holding 13th place on 33 points after 29 matches (GD -20) and Heracles rooted at 18th with just 19 points (GD -40). Both sides endured heavy defeats on April 5—Zwolle thrashed 5-0 by Go Ahead Eagles and Heracles routed 4-1 at Heerenveen—highlighting porous defenses amid Heracles' desperate survival fight. Zwolle benefit from home form and mid-table comfort, while Heracles eye revenge after their stunning 8-2 win over Zwolle in November 2025; key absences like Zwolle's Shola Shoretire (knee, out until early May) could influence lineups, with table positions and recent momentum shaping trader consensus on outcomes like win, goals, or clean sheets.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner leads trader consensus at 47.5% implied probability for the 2026 Wimbledon men's singles title, propelled by his dominant 28-2 record this season, including Monte-Carlo Masters victory over Carlos Alcaraz and a Madrid Open final run that reclaimed the ATP No. 1 ranking ahead of French Open. Alcaraz sits at 20.0% as the defending grass-court champion with strong surface history, though recent head-to-head losses to Sinner temper expectations. Novak Djokovic's 6.8% reflects ongoing shoulder injury sidelining him from Miami, Monte Carlo, and Madrid, dropping him to No. 4 and raising fitness doubts for the grass swing. Alexander Zverev at 3.5% benefits from No. 3 ranking and clay prowess, but limited Wimbledon success caps upside amid a competitive ATP field entering the short grass season post-Roland Garros.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) hit $8,200 (HIGH) in December?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and market close on the final day of trading for December 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPX) shows a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

eventMetadata.context_description: The S&P 500 index, hovering near 7,270 after a 10% advance over the past month and new closing highs, embodies trader consensus on sustained momentum fueled by robust Q1 earnings beats and projections for 20% growth in Q2 2026. The Federal Reserve's April 28-29 decision to maintain the federal funds rate at 3.50%-3.75%, amid steady 3.64% effective rates and moderating inflation, has bolstered risk appetite alongside falling Treasury yields. Polymarket's aggregated sentiment, backed by real capital, anticipates further upside through year-end, though the June 16-17 FOMC meeting and escalating Middle East tensions loom as pivotal catalysts that could shift rate path expectations and index trajectory.

Minnesota Wild sit at 19.2% following their 4-2 series victory over Dallas Stars, capped by a 5-2 Game 6 clincher yesterday that highlighted momentum from their central division clash. Second-round path favors Avalanche against Wild, underscoring skin-in-the-game crowd wisdom on Colorado's home-ice advantage and form.

Trader sentiment favors PSG due to their strong home form, dominant head-to-head record (21 wins in 35 meetings), and recent Ligue 1 victory over Toulouse (3-1), despite UCL demands and injuries sidelining Nuno Mendes (muscle) and Timothée Pembele (knee). Lorient's momentum from a shock 2-0 upset over fourth-placed Marseille on April 18 is tempered by key absences like Théo Le Bris (knee, early May return) and Mohamed Talbi (calf doubt), plus coach Olivier Pantaloni's announced season-end departure introducing uncertainty.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Amsterdam be 18°C on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Amsterdam Airport Schiphol Station in degrees Celsius on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Amsterdam Airport Schiphol Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/nl/schiphol/EHAM.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors a highest temperature of 20°C (52.5% implied probability) at Amsterdam Airport Schiphol on May 2, driven by the latest KNMI short-range forecasts and ECMWF/GFS ensemble means projecting 20-21°C under a high-pressure ridge promoting subsidence and warm air advection from the south. This exceeds early May climatological averages of 15-16°C, following a recent shift from cooler spring patterns. Afternoon thunderstorms with hail and gusty winds, per KNMI warnings, introduce cloud cover and convection risks that could suppress peaks, supporting secondary odds for 19°C (22.5%) and 21°C (18%). Watch 12Z model updates and afternoon observations for refined guidance amid typical forecast spread in such transitional setups.

markets.question: Will Iván Cepeda Castro place 2nd in the 1st round of the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
markets.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the results of the first round of the Colombian presidential election are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

Late April surveys—AtlasIntel (Apr 25-29: Cepeda 37.4%, de la Espriella 29.4%, Valencia 20.9%) and Guarumo-EcoAnalítica (Apr 22-29: Cepeda 38%, de la Espriella 23.9%, Valencia 22.8%)—show de la Espriella consolidating outsider momentum from his National Salvation Movement, while Valencia's Democratic Center backing faces right-wing vote splits. Alianza Verde's April 30 endorsement strengthens Cepeda's Pacto Histórico lead, tightening the battle for runoff qualification as campaigns intensify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Los Angeles Dodgers lead trader consensus at 29.5% implied probability to win the 2026 World Series, driven by their dominant early-season form atop power rankings with MLB's best run differential (+67), highest team batting average (.278), and most home runs (45), bolstered by offseason acquisition of Kyle Tucker adding outfield depth to stars like Ohtani and Betts. Atlanta Braves (11.1%) have climbed via a surging rotation and balanced lineup free of major injuries, while New York Yankees (10.5%) command AL East standings at 20-11 behind offensive firepower despite Giancarlo Stanton's low-grade calf strain. Seattle Mariners (5.9%) differentiate through elite pitching staff, Chicago Cubs (4.2%) via unexpected momentum, and Detroit Tigers (3.6%) with breakout performances; the wide-open field hinges on sustained health, bullpen strength, and schedule spots amid 30-game samples.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the #1 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Anthropic at 82% implied probability to hold the #1 large language model spot on the Chatbot Arena leaderboard by May's end, driven by Claude Opus 4.7's April 16 release, which solidified its lead across coding, search, and document benchmarks—outpacing rivals like OpenAI's newly launched GPT-5.5 (debuting April 23 but ranking second in key arenas) and Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro. Anthropic's consistent frontier performance in agentic tasks and long-context reasoning underpins this positioning, with no confirmed rival model releases slated before resolution. Google trails at 14% on multimodal strengths, while OpenAI and xAI linger below 3% amid integration delays; watch for unannounced drops or leaderboard volatility in the final weeks.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on EUR/USD reaching key 2026 levels hinges on persistent U.S.-Eurozone interest rate differentials, with the Federal Reserve holding the federal funds rate at 3.5%-3.75% in its April 28-29 meeting amid an 8-4 dissent—the highest in years—while the ECB maintained its 2.0% deposit facility rate on April 30 following Eurozone April CPI flashing at 3.0% year-over-year, up from 2.6%. U.S. March CPI at 3.3% underscores sticky inflation, supporting dollar strength despite current EUR/USD near 1.174; April U.S. CPI due May 12 could shift Fed cut expectations. Upcoming ECB June 11 and Fed June 16-17 meetings loom as catalysts, alongside GDP and labor data influencing policy divergence and exchange rate paths through year-end.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve maintained its federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% unchanged following the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting—the third consecutive hold—despite March CPI inflation surging to 3.3% year-over-year on a 0.9% monthly rise, exceeding forecasts, while unemployment held steady at 4.3%. The effective rate lingers at 3.64%, with Fed funds futures implying trader consensus for a mostly flat path through 2026 amid balanced risks to growth and price stability. Polymarket sentiment reflects skin-in-the-game bets on modest easing before year-end 2027 if disinflation materializes, though policy dissent signals debate. Watch April CPI on May 12 and June 16-17 FOMC projections for shifts in rate expectations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin contest for the halftime result in Chelsea's home Premier League clash with Nottingham Forest, with all outcomes at 50% amid widespread injury doubts on both sides. Chelsea, sitting sixth in the table, face defensive headaches without Reece James, Levi Colwill, and Trevoh Chalobah, while João Pedro and Estevão remain fitness concerns after recent thigh and hamstring issues; Enzo Fernández is expected back from a calf knock. Forest, buoyed by Europa League semifinal progress but ninth in standings, counter with their own absences including Chris Wood, Callum Hudson-Odoi, and Murillo, yet boast resilient away form that has frustrated top sides. Cautious opening exchanges in prior head-to-heads further even the implied probabilities, highlighting lineup uncertainties and mutual vulnerabilities.

markets.question: Will North and South Korea engage in direct talks by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, North and South Korea engage in direct talks, defined as an official meeting or communication between government representatives of both sides, conducted without a third-party relaying messages, by June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

The talks may be in-person, by phone, or virtual, and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by credible media. 

Routine military deconfliction, backchannel exchanges, or talks conducted entirely through another country or organization will not count. 

The resolutions source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 93% implied probability against direct talks between North and South Korea by June 30, driven by Pyongyang's entrenched hostility and prioritization of military advancements over dialogue. North Korea's multiple short-range ballistic missile tests on April 19 underscored ongoing escalation, while Kim Jong Un's March formalization of South Korea as its "primary hostile state" remains the dominant policy framework, blocking bilateral engagement. Recent high-level visits from Russian State Duma Chairman Vyacheslav Volodin on April 27 and Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi on April 10 highlight Pyongyang's deepening alliances with Moscow and Beijing, diverting focus from Seoul. South Korea's early April apology for a drone incursion elicited a mild North Korean response but no reciprocal overtures or scheduled negotiations, reinforcing structural barriers absent late-breaking diplomatic shifts.

markets.question: Will Israel win the televote for Eurovision 2026?
markets.description:
The Grand Final of the Eurovision Song Contest 2026 is scheduled for May 16, 2026.

This market will resolve to the country that receives the highest number of points from the televote in the Grand Final of the Eurovision Song Contest 2026.

All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Israel maintains a trader consensus edge at 33% implied probability for the Eurovision 2026 televote winner with Noam Bettan's "Michelle," fueled by back-to-back public vote triumphs in recent contests and robust diaspora mobilization evident in pre-party buzz and betting surges over the past week. Greece trails closely at 25.5% as Akylas's "Ferto" gains traction through viral promotional clips and anticipated high-energy staging highlighted in stand-in rehearsals three days ago, positioning it as a televote dark horse. Finland's Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen's "Liekinheitin" holds third at 19.5%, bolstered by broad fan polls but lagging in pure public sentiment per Polymarket's skin-in-the-game traders. First on-stage rehearsals begin today in Vienna, with semi-finals May 12 and 14 potentially shifting dynamics before the May 16 grand final.

markets.question: Elon Bull Run Parlay
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if all of the following conditions are met between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET:

- Elon Musk trillionaire
- Another Elon baby
- 9+ SpaceX Starship launches reach space

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will remain open until it is confirmed that at least one of the above conditions has not been met, or until all of the above conditions have been met within the specified timeframe.

The full rules for this market can be found here: 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Elon+Bull+Run+Parlay.pdf

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Elon Bull Run Parlay, with "No" at an 88% implied probability, reflects the compounded unlikelihood of all three 2026 milestones aligning: Elon Musk reaching trillionaire status per Bloomberg Billionaires Index, announcing another child, and SpaceX achieving 9+ Starship launches to 100 km altitude. As of early May, Musk's net worth hovers around $800 billion—buoyed by Tesla's $573 million in sales to xAI and SpaceX but far from doubling amid volatile markets—while no new baby has been announced. Starship progress lags with just one qualifying flight (Flight 12 on April 7), despite Flight 13 slated for early May and FAA approvals for up to 44 launches annually; historical cadence and technical hurdles temper expectations for the required pace. Recent Tesla-xAI investments signal synergies, but traders see slim odds for the full parlay by year-end.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 49.5% implied probability for South Korea's 2026 annual CPI inflation at 3.0% or higher, driven by persistent Middle East tensions—specifically the U.S.-Iran conflict—elevating oil prices and import costs, with March 2026 CPI accelerating to 2.2% year-over-year from February's 2.0%, though below the 2.4% forecast. Upward revisions from forecasters underscore risks: AMRO to 2.3%, OECD to 2.7%, and Bank of Korea's 2.1% projection amid a policy rate hold at 2.5% in April, as Governor signals data-dependent vigilance on inflation above the 2% target. Mid-2% bins (2.4-2.9%) capture 44% combined odds, reflecting balanced growth at 2% GDP forecast, ahead of April CPI data and May BOK meeting.

markets.question: Spread: Newcastle United FC (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for May 2 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Newcastle United FC" if Newcastle United FC win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Brighton & Hove Albion FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Newcastle United FC vs. Brighton & Hove Albion FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Premier League game, scheduled for May 2 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Newcastle United host Brighton & Hove Albion at St. James' Park in Premier League Matchweek 35, with both sides vying in the mid-table scrum—Newcastle around 14th after 33 games amid a tough run including recent losses to Crystal Palace and Bournemouth, while Brighton sit higher near 9th following a 2-2 draw at Tottenham. Newcastle's injury woes persist, with captain Bruno Guimarães pushing for a return from hamstring trouble and illness after two months out, though Fabian Schär's foot infection sidelines him into early May; Brighton's list is lighter despite Diego Gómez's recent knock. Home advantage and potential returns could boost the Magpies, but Brighton's stronger recent form and head-to-head edge, including a 2-1 win last October, shape trader consensus on a competitive clash.

markets.question: Will Mark Lynch be the Republican nominee for Senate in South Carolina?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from South Carolina.

If no 2026 South Carolina Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the South Carolina Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: South Carolina Republican Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from South Carolina.

If no 2026 South Carolina Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the South Carolina Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Sen. Lindsey Graham leads trader consensus at 85.5% implied probability to win South Carolina's June 9 Republican Senate primary, driven by his overwhelming fundraising edge—over $20 million raised and $21 million cash on hand as of late March—along with a presidential endorsement from Donald Trump and strong incumbency advantages in a deep-red state primary. Challenger Mark Lynch holds 10.5% following Paul Dans' April 10 withdrawal and endorsement, which consolidated anti-Graham support from the Project 2025 architect amid polls showing Graham at 41% to Lynch's 21% in March. Remaining candidates Paul Dans (0.8%) and Thomas Murphy (0.1%) trail as minor contenders, with trader odds reflecting Graham's resources to dominate the closing five-week sprint despite his 61% unfavorable rating in recent surveys. Late scandals or turnout surges could shift dynamics.
